text.title: PMBOK 8 AI Appendix and Adoption Data
text.summary: PMBOK 8 AI guidance, adoption rates by PM area, the DORA 2025 AI productivity paradox, and implementation approach.

rule.statement: Every AI-assisted PM decision must be logged with the format: "AI suggested [X] based on [data]; human PM approved/modified/rejected because [reason]."
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 AI appendix requires human oversight and traceability for AI-assisted decisions; undocumented AI suggestions are not auditable and cannot demonstrate responsible AI use.

rule.statement: Agents must surface decisions for human approval rather than acting autonomously on PM actions (reassigning resources, closing issues, changing deadlines).
rule.rationale: PMBOK 8 explicitly requires human control for AI-assisted decisions; autonomous PM actions by agents without human approval violates the standard's ethical AI guidance.

section.title: Adoption Rates by PM Area
section.p: 2024 adoption rates for AI in project management: Risk Management (predictive identification) 54%, Task Automation (routine workflows) 53%, Predictive Analysis (outcome forecasting) 52%, Schedule Optimization (timeline prediction) 52%, Resource Planning (capacity forecasting) 47%.

rule.statement: Start AI integration with the three highest-value, lowest-risk use cases: status report generation, meeting summary to action items, overdue task alerts.
rule.rationale: These use cases have clear output, easy human verification, and no irreversible consequences — they build team confidence in AI PM tools before tackling riskier automation like resource reallocation.

rule.statement: Measure end-to-end flow time (idea to production), not just individual task velocity — the AI productivity paradox is invisible when measuring only individual output.
rule.rationale: DORA 2025: AI tools produce 21% more tasks and 98% more PRs at individual level, but organizational delivery metrics stay flat because bottlenecks shift to review, integration, and deployment. Individual velocity metrics create a false sense of acceleration.

antipattern.description: Reporting AI productivity gains based on issue velocity or PR count alone.
antipattern.reason: These metrics improve while deployment frequency and lead time stay flat; management acts on misleading signals and invests in more AI tooling instead of fixing the review bottleneck.

section.title: Industry Results
section.p: Documented results: 96% prediction accuracy (Intelligent Project Prediction tool, specific construction domain), 15% reduction in budget overruns (Acciona, with sufficient historical data), 84% improved efficiency (Project.co survey, self-reported). All results require domain-specific historical data — generalizing to novel projects is unreliable.

rule.statement: ML risk prediction tools require 3–6 months of historical project data before predictions become reliable; do not deploy on novel project types without this baseline.
rule.rationale: Risk prediction models trained on insufficient or non-representative data produce confident-sounding but unreliable probability scores; this is more dangerous than no prediction because it suppresses human judgment.
